UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF DEFENDANT'S FINANCIAL CONDITION AND NET WORTH AFTER DEFAULT WAS ENTERED AND RELIEF THEREFROM WAS DENIED IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN BURDEN AND FAILED TO PROVIDE CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE OF STRONG LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON ITS CLAIM PURSUANT TO SECTION 3294 (FRAUD OPPRESSION OR MALICE) BY SUPPORTING AFFIDAVITS. MOVING PARTY'S RELIANCE ON ENTRY OF DEFAULT IS MISPLACED (MOTION 4:22-23). DEFAULT ADMITS ONLY WELL PLEADED FACTS ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF IN THE COMPLAINT, NOT CONTENTIONS, DEDUCTIONS, OR CONCLUSIONS OF FACT OR LAW. SEE KIM V. WESTMOORE PARTNERS, INC. (2011) 201 CAL.APP.4TH 267. PLAINTIFF'S CONSOLATORY ALLEGATIONS THAT DEFENDANT'S ACTS WERE "CARRIED OUT IN BAD FAITH FOR THE SPECIFIC PURPOSE OF INJURING PLAINTIFFS" (COMPLAINT PARAGRAPH 1); "FOR THE PURPOSE OF HARASSING" (COMPLAINT PARAGRAPH 33); AND "DONE INTENTIONALLY AND IN BAD FAITH" (COMPLAINT PARAGRAPH 42) IS NOT A SUBSTITUTE FOR PRESENTING "CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE" OF "SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY" OF PREVAILING BY "APPROPRIATE AFFIDAVITS." CCC 3294; CCP 3295(C). SINCE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN, THE COURT DOES NOT REACH THE ISSUE OF PROPRIETY OF OPPOSITION FILED BY DEFAULTED DEFENDANT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, REPORTER LUCY CARRILLO-GRUBBS, CSR #6766

Off Calendar Jan-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER AWARDING REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES, COSTS AND PENALTIES

Appearances: Karen Y. Uchiyama (415.563.9300) and Phillip T.S. Tukia of Maranga Morgenstern (415.248.5315) for defendant Paul Kraaijvanger; No other appearances.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED in part and denied in part. Motion for imposition of sanctions in the amount of $1,000 and for "other relief" per CCP 473(c)(1) is denied. Motion for recovery of reasonable compensatory legal fees is GRANTED. Karen Uchiyama shall remit to Plaintiff the sum of $5,575 representing reasonable amount of fees incurred in obtaining a prove-up judgment (executed on December 30, 2015). See Rogalski v. Nabers Cadillac (1992) 11 Cal.App.4th 816; Vanderkous v. Comley (2010) 188 Cal.App.4th 111. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR 13293 (DTI Global, Tel: 415.357.4300) (501/REQ)

Benjamin Martin, Esq. (510-227-4406) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Caitlin Fitting.
Karen Uchiyama, Esq. (415-563-9301) and Phillip Tukia, Esq. (415-248-5315) for Defendant/Moving party PAUL KRAAIJVANGER.
Court reporter: Dawn Stark, CSR #7847, 415-362-4346, Reported.

The Court announces the tentative ruling on all 7 motions. On motion #5, Defendant's motion to stay the only issue is the amount of the bond. On motion #6, Plaintiff's motion to strike, the Court denies motion.

Hearing is held. Defendant submits on the motion to stay. Both sides Argued. Plaintiff Argued Defendant has not met the burden per 917.1 of an appeal bond to stay the execution. The Court denies Plaintiff's request re 917.1. Plaintiff argues amount of cost bills. Defendant responds, objects to sur reply, request to strike sur reply and request supplemental briefing.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court has heard arguments on 2013 bill and sufficient amount of bond. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling on all 6 motions and rules as follows on Defendant's motion to stay.

DEFENDANT PAUL KRAAIJVANGER Notice Of Motion To Stay All Proceedings, Pending Appeal is taken under submission. The Court orders parties, no further motions to be filed until the Court has made a determination on this matter. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Defendant submits a proposed order. Notice Of Motion And Omtion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Deft'S Financial Condition And Net Worth After Default Was Entered And Relief Therefrom Was Denied;
Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Benjamin Martin, Esq. of Law Offices of Benjamin Martin, (415-558-1760), for Caitlin Fitting, Plaintiff and moving party / Karen Uchiyama, Esq. of Law Offices of Karen Uchiyama, (415-563-9301), for Paul Kraaijvanger, Defendant and opposing party / Court Reporter Lucy Carrillo-Grubbs, CSR#6766, (415-597-5600).

PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING's Motion For An Order Permitting Discovery Of Defendant's Financial Condition And Net Worth After Default Was Entered And Relief Therefrom Was Denied is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and failed to provide clear and convincing evidence of strong likelihood of prevailing on its claim pursuant to Section 3294 (fraud oppression or malice) by supporting affidavits. Moving party's reliance on entry of default is misplaced (Motion 4:22-23). Default admits only well pleaded facts alleged by plaintiff in the complaint, not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. See Kim v. Westmoore Partners, Inc. (2011) 201 Cal.App.4th 267. Plaintiff's consolatory allegations that defendant's acts were "carried out in bad faith for the specific purpose of injuring Plaintiffs" (Complaint Paragraph 1); "for the purpose of harassing" (Complaint Paragraph 33); and "done intentionally and in bad faith" (Complaint Paragraph 42) is not a substitute for presenting "clear and convincing evidence" of "substantial probability" of prevailing by "appropriate affidavits." CCC 3294; CCP 3295(c). Since Plaintiff failed to sustain its initial burden, the Court does not reach the issue of propriety of opposition filed by defaulted defendant. Prevailing party to prepare order approved as to form. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Reporter Lucy Carrillo-Grubbs, CSR #6766, (D501)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Mtn For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Uchiyama, Esq. (415-563-9301) of Law Offices of Karen Uchiyama, for Paul Kraaijvanger, Defendant and moving party / Benjamin Martin, Esq. (415-558-1760) of Law Offices of Benjamin Martin, appearing through court call for Caitlin Fitting, Plaintiff and opposing party.

ARGUED - Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT PAUL KRAAIJVANGER and REAL EQUITY GROUP ONE, LLC's Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Denying Motion For Mandatory Relief From Entry Of Default is GRANTED, in part, and DENIED, in part, as follows:
(1) The Court will reconsider its prior order based on new fact, specifically, even though courtesy copy provided to the Court did not contain proposed demurrer referenced in paragraph 47 of Karen Uchiyama Declaration, such pleading was attached to the original filed with the Court.
(2) The Court DENIES Paul Kraaijvanger's Motion to Set Aside Default. Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) provides that "The court shall, whenever an application for relief is made no more than six months after entry of judgment, is in proper form, and is accompanied by an attorney's sworn affidavit attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect, vacate any 'resulting default, 'unless the court finds that the default or dismissal was not in fact caused by the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." The Court does not find that default was caused by attorney mistake, inadvertence, surprise or neglect. If an "'intentional strategic decision'caused the default section 473 relief is not available." Jerry's Shell v. Equilon Enterprises, LLC, (2005)134 Cal. App. 4th 1058, 1073. Newly provided explanation for delay in seeking relief supports Court's finding. Holding that counsel's actions (See Uchiyama Declaration in Support of Motion to Set Side Default paragraph 9-10 and Reply in support of Motion for Reconsideration 2:10-15) were subject to automatic, mandatory relief, would reward and encourage this conduct and render the latter part of the statute quoted above superfluous/meaningless, i.e. "unless the court finds that the default or dismissal was not in fact caused by the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect." Order is signed in open court. Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
